title: What is MySQL?
description: Select the correct definition of MySQL.
options: An open-source relational database management system
correct_answer: An open-source relational database management system

title: What does SQL stand for?
description: Choose the correct expansion of SQL.
correct_answer: Structured Query Language

title: What is a primary key?
description: What does a primary key do?
options: Uniquely identifies each record in a table
correct_answer: Uniquely identifies each record in a table

title: What is a foreign key?
description: What is the purpose of a foreign key?
options: References a primary key in another table to establish a relationship
correct_answer: References a primary key in another table to establish a relationship

title: What is a SELECT statement?
options: Retrieves data from one or more tables
correct_answer: Retrieves data from one or more tables

title: What is the WHERE clause?
description: What does WHERE do in a query?
options: Filters rows based on a condition
correct_answer: Filters rows based on a condition

title: What is INSERT INTO?
options: Adds new rows to a table
correct_answer: Adds new rows to a table

title: What is UPDATE?
description: What does the UPDATE statement do?
options: Modifies existing records in a table
correct_answer: Modifies existing records in a table

title: What is DELETE?
description: What does the DELETE statement do?
correct_answer: Removes rows from a table

title: What is CREATE TABLE?
options: Defines a new table with columns and constraints
correct_answer: Defines a new table with columns and constraints

title: What is DROP TABLE?
options: Permanently removes a table and its data
correct_answer: Permanently removes a table and its data

title: What is ALTER TABLE?
options: Modifies the structure of an existing table
correct_answer: Modifies the structure of an existing table

title: What is ORDER BY?
options: Sorts the result set by one or more columns
correct_answer: Sorts the result set by one or more columns

title: What is GROUP BY?
options: Groups rows sharing a value and allows aggregate functions
correct_answer: Groups rows sharing a value and allows aggregate functions

title: What is COUNT()?
options: The number of rows matching the criteria
correct_answer: The number of rows matching the criteria

title: What is SUM()?
options: Returns the total sum of a numeric column
correct_answer: Returns the total sum of a numeric column

title: What is AVG()?
options: The average value of a numeric column
correct_answer: The average value of a numeric column

title: What is MAX()?
options: The highest value in a column
correct_answer: The highest value in a column

title: What is MIN()?
options: The lowest value in a column
correct_answer: The lowest value in a column

title: What is DISTINCT?
correct_answer: Returns only unique values

title: What is LIMIT?
description: What does the LIMIT clause do?
options: Restricts the number of rows returned
correct_answer: Restricts the number of rows returned

title: What is LIKE?
description: What does the LIKE operator do?
correct_answer: Pattern matching with % and _ wildcards

title: What is BETWEEN?
correct_answer: Selects values within a range (inclusive)

title: What is IN?
description: What does the IN operator do?
options: Checks if a value is within a set of specified values
correct_answer: Checks if a value is within a set of specified values

title: What is NULL?
description: What does NULL represent in MySQL?
correct_answer: A missing or unknown value

title: What is IS NULL?
description: How do you check for NULL values?
correct_answer: WHERE column IS NULL

title: What is AUTO_INCREMENT?
options: Automatically generates a unique number for new rows
correct_answer: Automatically generates a unique number for new rows

title: What is VARCHAR?
correct_answer: Variable-length character strings

title: What is INT?
correct_answer: Integer (whole number) values

title: What is BOOLEAN in MySQL?
options: BOOLEAN is an alias for TINYINT(1)
correct_answer: BOOLEAN is an alias for TINYINT(1)

title: What is TEXT?
options: Large string data up to 65,535 characters
correct_answer: Large string data up to 65,535 characters

title: What is DATE?
correct_answer: YYYY-MM-DD

title: What is TIMESTAMP?
options: Date and time, auto-updating on row changes
correct_answer: Date and time, auto-updating on row changes

title: What is TRUNCATE?
options: Removes all rows from a table quickly (DDL operation)
correct_answer: Removes all rows from a table quickly (DDL operation)

title: What is HAVING?
description: What does the HAVING clause do?
options: Filters groups after GROUP BY (used with aggregate functions)
correct_answer: Filters groups after GROUP BY (used with aggregate functions)

title: What is an alias in MySQL?
options: Gives a temporary name to a table or column
correct_answer: Gives a temporary name to a table or column

title: What is CONCAT()?
options: Joins two or more strings together
correct_answer: Joins two or more strings together

title: What is NOW()?
correct_answer: The current date and time

title: What is IFNULL()?
options: Returns expr1 if it is not NULL, otherwise returns expr2
correct_answer: Returns expr1 if it is not NULL, otherwise returns expr2

title: What is UNIQUE constraint?
options: All values in a column are distinct
correct_answer: All values in a column are distinct

title: What is NOT NULL?
options: Ensures a column cannot have NULL values
correct_answer: Ensures a column cannot have NULL values

title: What is DEFAULT?
description: What does DEFAULT do in column definition?
options: Sets a default value for a column when no value is provided
correct_answer: Sets a default value for a column when no value is provided

title: What is a database schema?
description: What is a schema in MySQL?
options: The structure that defines tables, columns, types, and relationships
correct_answer: The structure that defines tables, columns, types, and relationships

title: What is SHOW TABLES?
options: Lists all tables in the current database
correct_answer: Lists all tables in the current database

title: What is DESCRIBE?
options: Shows the structure (columns, types, constraints) of a table
correct_answer: Shows the structure (columns, types, constraints) of a table

title: What is ENUM in MySQL?
options: Restricts a column to a predefined set of values
correct_answer: Restricts a column to a predefined set of values

title: What is JSON type in MySQL?
description: What does the JSON data type store?
options: JSON documents that can be validated and queried
correct_answer: JSON documents that can be validated and queried

title: What is a DECIMAL type?
options: For exact numeric values like currency
correct_answer: For exact numeric values like currency

title: What is FLOAT vs DOUBLE?
options: FLOAT uses 4 bytes (single precision); DOUBLE uses 8 bytes (double precision)
correct_answer: FLOAT uses 4 bytes (single precision); DOUBLE uses 8 bytes (double precision)

title: What is a JOIN?
description: What does a JOIN do in MySQL?
options: Combines rows from two or more tables based on a related column
correct_answer: Combines rows from two or more tables based on a related column

title: What is an INNER JOIN?
description: What rows does INNER JOIN return?
options: Only rows with matching values in both tables
Only rows from the left table
correct_answer: Only rows with matching values in both tables

title: What is a LEFT JOIN?
options: All rows from the left table and matching rows from the right (NULL for no match)
correct_answer: All rows from the left table and matching rows from the right (NULL for no match)

title: What is a RIGHT JOIN?
options: All rows from the right table and matching rows from the left (NULL for no match)
correct_answer: All rows from the right table and matching rows from the left (NULL for no match)

title: What is a CROSS JOIN?
options: The Cartesian product of both tables
correct_answer: The Cartesian product of both tables

title: What is a self join?
options: A table joined with itself using aliases
correct_answer: A table joined with itself using aliases

title: What is a subquery?
description: What is a subquery in MySQL?
options: A query nested inside another query
correct_answer: A query nested inside another query

title: What is a correlated subquery?
description: How does a correlated subquery differ from a regular one?
options: It references columns from the outer query and executes once per outer row
It is faster than regular subqueries
correct_answer: It references columns from the outer query and executes once per outer row

title: What is an INDEX?
options: Speeds up data retrieval at the cost of extra storage and slower writes
correct_answer: Speeds up data retrieval at the cost of extra storage and slower writes

title: What is a composite index?
options: An index on a single column
correct_answer: An index on multiple columns

title: What is EXPLAIN?
options: Shows the execution plan for a query
correct_answer: Shows the execution plan for a query

title: What is normalization?
description: What is the purpose of database normalization?
options: Reduces data redundancy and dependency by organizing tables
correct_answer: Reduces data redundancy and dependency by organizing tables

title: What is 1NF?
description: What does First Normal Form require?
options: Each column holds atomic (indivisible) values and each record is unique
correct_answer: Each column holds atomic (indivisible) values and each record is unique

title: What is 2NF?
description: What does Second Normal Form require?
options: 1NF plus no partial dependencies on composite primary keys
correct_answer: 1NF plus no partial dependencies on composite primary keys

title: What is 3NF?
description: What does Third Normal Form require?
correct_answer: 2NF plus no transitive dependencies

title: What is denormalization?
description: Why would you denormalize a database?
options: To improve read performance by adding redundancy
correct_answer: To improve read performance by adding redundancy

title: What is a VIEW?
description: What is a VIEW in MySQL?
options: A virtual table based on the result of a query
correct_answer: A virtual table based on the result of a query

title: What is a stored procedure?
options: A precompiled set of SQL statements stored in the database
correct_answer: A precompiled set of SQL statements stored in the database

title: What is a trigger?
description: What is a trigger in MySQL?
options: A set of SQL statements automatically executed in response to a table event
correct_answer: A set of SQL statements automatically executed in response to a table event

title: What is a transaction?
options: A group of operations that either all succeed or all fail (atomicity)
correct_answer: A group of operations that either all succeed or all fail (atomicity)

title: What are ACID properties?
correct_answer: Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability

title: What is COMMIT?
options: Saves all changes made during the current transaction permanently
correct_answer: Saves all changes made during the current transaction permanently

title: What is ROLLBACK?
options: Undoes all changes made during the current transaction
correct_answer: Undoes all changes made during the current transaction

title: What is UNION?
options: Combines result sets of two or more SELECT statements (removes duplicates)
correct_answer: Combines result sets of two or more SELECT statements (removes duplicates)

title: What is UNION ALL?
description: How does UNION ALL differ from UNION?
options: UNION ALL keeps duplicate rows; UNION removes them
correct_answer: UNION ALL keeps duplicate rows; UNION removes them

title: What is a CTE?
description: What is a Common Table Expression?
options: A temporary named result set defined with WITH that exists for a single query
correct_answer: A temporary named result set defined with WITH that exists for a single query

title: What is GROUP_CONCAT()?
options: Concatenates values from multiple rows into a single string
correct_answer: Concatenates values from multiple rows into a single string

title: What is COALESCE()?
options: The first non-NULL value in the argument list
correct_answer: The first non-NULL value in the argument list

title: What is CASE WHEN?
options: Provides conditional logic in SQL queries, similar to if-else
correct_answer: Provides conditional logic in SQL queries, similar to if-else

title: What is a FULLTEXT index?
description: What does a FULLTEXT index enable?
options: Efficient text searching with MATCH AGAINST syntax
correct_answer: Efficient text searching with MATCH AGAINST syntax

title: What are window functions?
options: Perform calculations across a set of rows related to the current row without grouping
correct_answer: Perform calculations across a set of rows related to the current row without grouping

title: What is ROW_NUMBER()?
options: Assigns a unique sequential integer to each row within a partition
correct_answer: Assigns a unique sequential integer to each row within a partition

title: What is RANK() vs DENSE_RANK()?
options: RANK() leaves gaps after ties; DENSE_RANK() does not
correct_answer: RANK() leaves gaps after ties; DENSE_RANK() does not

title: What is the difference between DELETE and TRUNCATE?
options: DELETE is DML (can be rolled back with WHERE); TRUNCATE is DDL (removes all rows, faster, resets AUTO_INCREMENT)
correct_answer: DELETE is DML (can be rolled back with WHERE); TRUNCATE is DDL (removes all rows, faster, resets AUTO_INCREMENT)

title: What is REPLACE INTO?
options: Inserts a row or replaces it if a duplicate key exists
correct_answer: Inserts a row or replaces it if a duplicate key exists

title: What is INSERT ... ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE?
description: How does ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE work?
options: Inserts a row; if a duplicate key violation occurs, updates the existing row
correct_answer: Inserts a row; if a duplicate key violation occurs, updates the existing row

title: What is a covering index?
description: What makes an index a covering index?
options: It contains all columns needed by a query, avoiding table lookups
correct_answer: It contains all columns needed by a query, avoiding table lookups

title: What is the difference between CHAR and VARCHAR?
options: CHAR is fixed-length (padded with spaces); VARCHAR is variable-length
correct_answer: CHAR is fixed-length (padded with spaces); VARCHAR is variable-length

title: What is the InnoDB storage engine?
options: ACID transactions, row-level locking, foreign keys, crash recovery
correct_answer: ACID transactions, row-level locking, foreign keys, crash recovery

title: What is the difference between InnoDB and MyISAM?
options: InnoDB supports transactions and row locking; MyISAM doesn't but is faster for read-heavy workloads
correct_answer: InnoDB supports transactions and row locking; MyISAM doesn't but is faster for read-heavy workloads

title: What is a deadlock?
description: What is a deadlock in MySQL?
options: Two or more transactions waiting for each other to release locks, causing a circular wait
correct_answer: Two or more transactions waiting for each other to release locks, causing a circular wait

title: What are isolation levels?
description: Name the SQL standard isolation levels.
options: Read Uncommitted, Read Committed, Repeatable Read, Serializable
correct_answer: Read Uncommitted, Read Committed, Repeatable Read, Serializable

title: What is the default isolation level in MySQL?
description: Which isolation level does InnoDB use by default?
correct_answer: REPEATABLE READ

title: What is a prepared statement?
description: What are prepared statements used for?
options: Pre-compiled SQL queries that prevent SQL injection and improve performance
correct_answer: Pre-compiled SQL queries that prevent SQL injection and improve performance

title: What is LATERAL join?
description: What does LATERAL allow in MySQL 8.0?
options: A derived table that references columns from preceding tables in the FROM clause
correct_answer: A derived table that references columns from preceding tables in the FROM clause

title: What is EXISTS?
description: What does EXISTS do in a subquery?
options: Returns TRUE if the subquery returns at least one row
correct_answer: Returns TRUE if the subquery returns at least one row

title: Explain query optimization with EXPLAIN.
description: What key fields in EXPLAIN output should you examine for optimization?
options: type (join type), key (index used), rows (estimated rows), Extra (Using temporary, Using filesort)
correct_answer: type (join type), key (index used), rows (estimated rows), Extra (Using temporary, Using filesort)

title: What is the B+ tree index in MySQL?
description: How does B+ tree indexing work?
options: A balanced tree where all values are stored in leaf nodes with pointers for range scans
correct_answer: A balanced tree where all values are stored in leaf nodes with pointers for range scans

title: What is the clustered vs non-clustered index?
description: How do they differ in InnoDB?
options: Clustered index determines physical row order (primary key); non-clustered has a separate structure pointing to the clustered key
correct_answer: Clustered index determines physical row order (primary key); non-clustered has a separate structure pointing to the clustered key

title: What is the query optimizer in MySQL?
options: Determines the most efficient execution plan by evaluating join orders, indexes, and access methods
correct_answer: Determines the most efficient execution plan by evaluating join orders, indexes, and access methods

title: What is MVCC?
options: Each transaction sees a snapshot of data at a point in time, allowing reads without locking
correct_answer: Each transaction sees a snapshot of data at a point in time, allowing reads without locking

title: What is a gap lock in InnoDB?
description: What does a gap lock prevent?
options: Prevents inserts into the gap between index records, avoiding phantom reads
correct_answer: Prevents inserts into the gap between index records, avoiding phantom reads

title: What is a phantom read?
options: A transaction re-executes a query and sees new rows inserted by another committed transaction
correct_answer: A transaction re-executes a query and sees new rows inserted by another committed transaction

title: How does MySQL handle replication?
description: What types of replication does MySQL support?
options: Statement-based, row-based, and mixed replication from master to replicas via binary log
correct_answer: Statement-based, row-based, and mixed replication from master to replicas via binary log

title: What is the slow query log?
description: What does the slow query log capture?
options: Queries that exceed long_query_time threshold for performance analysis
correct_answer: Queries that exceed long_query_time threshold for performance analysis

title: What is partitioning in MySQL?
options: Splits a large table into smaller, manageable parts while appearing as one table
correct_answer: Splits a large table into smaller, manageable parts while appearing as one table

title: What is the redo log in InnoDB?
description: What does the redo log do?
options: Records changes for crash recovery, ensuring durability by replaying committed transactions
correct_answer: Records changes for crash recovery, ensuring durability by replaying committed transactions

title: What is the buffer pool?
description: What is the InnoDB buffer pool?
options: An in-memory cache for table and index data, reducing disk I/O
correct_answer: An in-memory cache for table and index data, reducing disk I/O

title: Write a query to find the second highest salary.
description: Write a MySQL query to find the second highest salary from an employees table with columns: id, name, salary.
correct_answer: SELECT MAX(salary) AS second_highest FROM employees WHERE salary < (SELECT MAX(salary) FROM employees);

title: Write a query to find duplicate records.
description: Write a query to find all duplicate email addresses in a users table.
correct_answer: SELECT email, COUNT(*) as count FROM users GROUP BY email HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;

title: Write a query using window functions.
description: Write a query to rank employees by salary within each department using DENSE_RANK().
correct_answer: SELECT name, department, salary, DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY department ORDER BY salary DESC) AS rank_num FROM employees;

title: Write a recursive CTE.
description: Write a recursive CTE to generate numbers from 1 to 10.
correct_answer: WITH RECURSIVE nums AS (SELECT 1 AS n UNION ALL SELECT n + 1 FROM nums WHERE n < 10) SELECT n FROM nums;

title: Write a pivot query.
description: Write a query to pivot monthly sales data so each month becomes a column. Table: sales(id, month, amount).
correct_answer: SELECT id, SUM(CASE WHEN month = 'Jan' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS Jan, SUM(CASE WHEN month = 'Feb' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS Feb, SUM(CASE WHEN month = 'Mar' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS Mar FROM sales GROUP BY id;
